meta.title: lazyit — self-hosted IT management for small teams
meta.description: A self-hosted system of record for small IT teams: assets, access, consumables and a knowledge base. Asset-centric, auditable by default, with a zero-knowledge secret manager.

hero.title: Everything your IT team owns, on the record.
hero.subtitle: A self-hosted system of record for small teams — assets, access, consumables and knowledge. Asset-centric, auditable by default, and yours to run.

thesis.title: Assets persist. People rotate.
thesis.body: Most tools hang ownership off a user. lazyit inverts it: the asset is the first-class citizen, and ownership is a timestamped record — who holds it, since when, and everything that changed. Someone leaves; the history stays intact.
thesis.p1: Ownership is a dated relation, never a column — so history is automatic.
thesis.p2: Every assignment, return and status change is on the timeline.
thesis.p3: Nothing is hard-deleted; the record can always answer “who had this, when”.

showcase.title: The whole estate, at a glance.
showcase.subtitle: Inventory, access and activity in one calm, dense view — built for operators, not dashboards-as-decoration.

pillars.title: One opinionated tool. Four jobs.
pillars.subtitle: Curated capability over a wall of toggles.

inventory.name: Inventory
inventory.desc: Assets and consumables together — models, locations, specs, and stock with an append-only movement ledger.

access.name: Access
access.desc: Who can reach which application — granted and revoked with a full audit trail.

knowledge.name: Knowledge
knowledge.desc: A searchable, versioned knowledge base for runbooks and how-tos.

manage.name: Manage
manage.desc: Users and locations, with roles and offboarding that returns every asset.

secrets.title: Secrets the server can't read.
secrets.body: The Secret Manager encrypts everything in your browser. lazyit stores only ciphertext and never holds your keys — the server literally cannot decrypt your vaults.
secrets.p1: Client-side encryption — the server only ever sees ciphertext.
secrets.p3: For credentials too sensitive for a shared doc.

selfhost.title: Runs inside your shop.
selfhost.body: One instance per organization, on your hardware. Inventory, access and credential-adjacent records are sensitive — they belong on-premises, not in someone else's cloud.
selfhost.p1: Docker Compose up — Postgres, Valkey, done.
selfhost.p2: No telemetry, no phone-home, no per-seat billing.
selfhost.p3: Your data stays yours, end to end.

cta.title: Stand up lazyit in an afternoon.
cta.body: Self-hosted, opinionated, IT-native. Set it up and watch your inventory take shape.

footer.tagline: Self-hosted IT management for small teams.
footer.note: No telemetry · Your data stays yours.
